claim.hypothesis: inverse relationship between severe psychological distress and motivation for distance learning
claim.statement: The multinomial logistic regression revealed that there was a statistically significant inverse relationship between severe psychological distress and motivation for distance learning (OR=0.45, P<0.0001, 95% CI: 0.28-0.7).
claim.statement_location: Section: 4 Results; Subsection: 4.3 Motivation for Distance Learning; p. 13.

data.access_details: The dataset generated and analyzed for this study are available from the corresponding author on a reasonable request
data.notes: Survey conducted in May 2020 in Jordan. Recruitment took place on Facebook and Whatsapp. Originally there were 397 questionnaires received but 16 were excluded due to incompleteness.

method.description: Descriptive analysis including comparison of means and non-parametric tests (like the Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test) were used along with multinomial logistic regression analysis to study the effect of psychological distress on distance learning motivation.
method.steps: (1) Recruit participants via Facebook and Whatsapp; (2) Have participants fill out the online questionnaire; (3) Clean the data and construct the variables; (4) Look at the descriptive analysis by comparing shares and means; (5) Run the multinomial logistic analysis.
method.independent_variables: Psychological distress levels (Mild, Moderate, and Severe compared to the reference group None).
method.tools_software: Statistical Package for Social Sciences (SPSS) version 23.0

results.summary: The multinomial logistic regression revealed that there was a statistically significant inverse relationship between severe psychological distress and motivation for distance learning (OR=0.45, P<0.0001, 95% CI: 0.28-0.7).

numerical_results.outcome_name: Degree of motivation for distance learning

metadata.original_paper_title: Is it Just About Physical Health? An Internet-Based Cross-Sectional Study Exploring the Psychological Impacts of COVID-19 Pandemic on University Students in Jordan Using Kessler Psychological Distress Scale
